findings.detail: A primary key is required for row-level uniqueness, audit log referencing (record_id FK), and reliable replication/CDC pipelines.
findings.remediation: ALTER TABLE country_codes ADD COLUMN id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY;

findings.detail: Table 'customers' does not have a 'updated_at' column. SAMA CSF §3.3.5 requires full audit trail metadata on all tables.
findings.remediation: ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW();

findings.message: No audit log table found for 'customers'
findings.detail: Table 'customers' contains Tier [1, 3] data but 'customers_audit_log' does not exist in the schema.
findings.remediation:
CREATE TABLE customers_audit_log (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    record_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
    operation VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL CHECK (operation IN ('INSERT','UPDATE','DELETE')),
    changed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    changed_by VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    old_values JSONB,
    new_values JSONB
);

findings.message: No audit log table found for 'accounts'
findings.detail: Table 'accounts' contains Tier [3] data but 'accounts_audit_log' does not exist in the schema.

CREATE TABLE accounts_audit_log (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,

);

findings.message: Tier 1 column 'full_name' is not stored as an encrypted type (current: 'varchar')
findings.detail: Column 'customers.full_name' is classified as Tier 1 (Directly Identifying). For postgres, encrypted storage types are: ['bytea']. PDPL Article 8 requires sensitive personal data to be encrypted at rest.

rule_results.rule_name: No Cascade Delete on Sensitive Tables

findings.rule_name: No Cascade Delete on Sensitive Tables
findings.detail: Table 'customers' contains Tier [1, 3] data. CASCADE DELETE would permanently erase regulated records and violates SAMA data retention requirements.
findings.remediation:
-- Change ON DELETE CASCADE to ON DELETE RESTRICT:
-- Drop and recreate the FK constraint on accounts(customer_id)
-- to reference customers with ON DELETE RESTRICT.

findings.message: consent_records table is missing from the schema
findings.detail: PDPL Article 10 requires that consent be recorded with the data subject's identity, purpose, and date. A consent_records table must be present.
findings.remediation:
CREATE TABLE consent_records (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    data_subject_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
    purpose VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    consent_given BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    consent_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    withdrawal_date TIMESTAMP NULL,
    consent_version VARCHAR(50),
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP,
    updated_by VARCHAR(255)
);

findings.message: data_subject_requests table is missing from the schema
findings.detail: PDPL Articles 14–18 grant data subjects the right to access, rectify, erase, and port their data. A data_subject_requests table is required to track these requests and their fulfilment.
findings.remediation:
CREATE TABLE data_subject_requests (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    data_subject_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
    request_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
        CHECK (request_type IN ('ACCESS','RECTIFICATION','ERASURE','PORTABILITY','RESTRICTION','OBJECTION')),
    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
    requested_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    completed_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
    response_due_date DATE NULL,
    notes TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP,
    updated_by VARCHAR(255)
);

findings.detail: Table 'customers' contains Tier 3 (Financial/Regulated) data. SAMA requires financial records to be retained for a minimum of 5 years. Adding 'retention_period' enables per-record retention policy enforcement.
findings.remediation: ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN retention_period SMALLINT NULL DEFAULT 60; -- months

findings.detail: Table 'customers' contains Tier 3 (Financial/Regulated) data. SAMA requires financial records to be retained for a minimum of 5 years. Adding 'data_category' enables per-record retention policy enforcement.
findings.remediation: ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN data_category VARCHAR(100) NULL;

findings.detail: Table 'accounts' contains Tier 3 (Financial/Regulated) data. SAMA requires financial records to be retained for a minimum of 5 years. Adding 'retention_period' enables per-record retention policy enforcement.
findings.remediation: ALTER TABLE accounts ADD COLUMN retention_period SMALLINT NULL DEFAULT 60; -- months

findings.detail: Table 'accounts' contains Tier 3 (Financial/Regulated) data. SAMA requires financial records to be retained for a minimum of 5 years. Adding 'data_category' enables per-record retention policy enforcement.
findings.remediation: ALTER TABLE accounts ADD COLUMN data_category VARCHAR(100) NULL;

findings.message: Transaction table 'transactions' missing a trace ID column (transaction_id, reference_id, correlation_id)
findings.detail: SAMA Open Banking Framework requires end-to-end traceability for all financial transactions. At least one of ['transaction_id', 'reference_id', 'correlation_id'] must be present.
findings.remediation: ALTER TABLE transactions ADD COLUMN reference_id VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL;

findings.message: Transaction table 'transactions' missing a status column
findings.detail: A status column is required to track transaction lifecycle (PENDING → PROCESSING → COMPLETED/FAILED) for SAMA compliance.
findings.remediation: ALTER TABLE transactions ADD COLUMN status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING';
